text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. They involve having someone else check your code before it’s added to the main project. This practice helps catch bugs early, when they are easier and cheaper to fix. Reviews also allow team members to share their expertise, as more experienced developers can suggest better approaches. Over time, this knowledge sharing helps everyone improve their skills. Ultimately, code reviews contribute to higher team quality. When code is carefully reviewed, the final product becomes more reliable and consistent, and the whole team feels more confident about their work.
